title: Who was Netflix's next live roast subject after Tom Brady?
rules: If Kevin Hart is or if it is announced that Kevin Hart is the the primary target of the roast and whose name appears in the official title or promotional materials of the roast special at the next Netflix-produced live or recorded celebrity roast special released on Netflix, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief:
I'll research this event to produce an evidence brief for a forecasting agent.## EVIDENCE BRIEF: Netflix's Next Live Roast Subject After Tom Brady

**Background and Track Record**

Netflix's "The Greatest Roast of All Time: Tom Brady" aired live on May 5, 2024, becoming a major cultural phenomenon. The special garnered 26 million views, spent three weeks in Netflix's Global Top 10, and earned an Emmy nomination for Outstanding Variety Special (Variety, 2026). Kevin Hart hosted the Brady roast, which was produced by Jeff Ross and Casey Patterson. Following this success, speculation immediately emerged about Netflix's next roast subject. **Official Announcement**

As of March 2026, Netflix officially announced that Kevin Hart will be the subject of its next live roast special. [redacted] Shane Gillis, a comedian with a Netflix scripted series, will host the event. Hart will executive produce alongside returning producers Jeff Ross and Casey Patterson. **Kevin Hart's Positioning**

Hart brings unique credentials to this roast. He previously served as host for the Brady roast and has extensive experience in the roast format. [redacted] Hart publicly expressed enthusiasm on Instagram, stating he was ready for the challenge. **Production Context**

Jeff Ross indicated in November 2025 (Variety) that Netflix wanted to maintain roasts as premium events, stating "I want it to feel like the Olympics — every year or two there's a gigantic roast." He noted that many B-list celebrities wanted to participate but that Netflix was selective about subjects. The Brady roast took three years to arrange, suggesting Netflix's deliberate approach to choosing targets. [redacted] (2) The roast will be produced by the same team (Jeff Ross, Casey Patterson) that created the Emmy-nominated Brady special, suggesting Netflix's confidence in this format continuation. (3) Hart's dual role as former roast host and current subject represents a notable format evolution for Netflix's live roast strategy.

args.reasoning:
Multiple search results converge on a clear, specific answer: Netflix announced "The Roast of Kevin Hart" as the next live roast special following the 2024 Tom Brady roast. The event is scheduled for May 10, 2026, at The Kia Forum in Los Angeles, as part of the Netflix Is a Joke Fest, hosted by Shane Gillis. Kevin Hart's name explicitly appears in the official title ("The Roast of Kevin Hart"), directly satisfying the resolution criteria.

Evidence inventory:
- STRONG: Variety, Hollywood Reporter, and Netflix's official page all confirm Kevin Hart as the next roast subject with the title "The Roast of Kevin Hart."
- STRONG: Confirmed event date (May 10/11, 2026) and venue (Kia Forum).
- STRONG: Shane Gillis (one of the listed outcomes) is the HOST, not the subject — so he is not the answer despite being mentioned.

Implication: Evidence shows Kevin Hart is named in the official title, therefore this market resolves YES for Kevin Hart with near-certainty. The other named comedians (Theo Von, Schulz, Gillis, etc.) are likely participants/roasters but not the subject. Given that the announcement is publicly made and an official title exists, this is effectively resolved.

I'll assign ~0.93 to Kevin Hart (leaving small mass for the unlikely chance an earlier/different roast special airs first or the announcement gets changed) and distribute small residual probability across other candidates, with Shane Gillis getting slightly more due to being involved (though as host, not subject).

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory and implications:
- Kevin Hart — STRONG: Multiple late-February/early-March 2026 reports indicate Netflix named Kevin Hart as the next “Greatest Roast of All Time” subject after Tom Brady. Evidence shows a concrete post-Brady announcement tying Hart to the GROAT brand, therefore he is highly likely to be the next roast target. MODERATE: Deep Netflix relationship (e.g., Lift in 2024; long-running HartBeat/Netflix pipeline; multiple Netflix stand-up specials). This shows aligned incentives and deal-making ease, therefore higher plausibility. MODERATE: Prior roast pedigree (Roastmaster for Comedy Central’s Justin Bieber Roast, 2015) shows format fit, therefore execution risk is low.
- Elon Musk — MODERATE negative: No credible reports linking Musk to a Netflix roast; brand/political risk for Netflix is high. Evidence shows absence of concrete movement and higher reputational risk, therefore low probability.
- Taylor Swift — STRONG negative: Brand-protective posture and no roast history; no announcements. Evidence shows low willingness to participate, therefore very low probability.
- Drake — MODERATE: Massive cultural draw and recent high-visibility rap beefs fuel roastable material, but no Netflix-linked reporting. Therefore small but nonzero chance.
- Joe Rogan — STRONG negative: Spotify exclusivity and limited Netflix ties, therefore unlikely.
- Logan Paul/Jake Paul — MODERATE: High-ROI spectacle candidates; Jake has prior Netflix doc (Untold). But no concrete roast signals, therefore modest tail risk each.
- MrBeast — STRONG negative: Publicly announced Amazon/Prime Video deal for Beast Games in 2024, therefore platform conflict and very low probability.
- Theo Von/Shane Gillis/Andrew Schulz — MODERATE: Have Netflix stand-up presence, but GROAT aims for broad mainstream names; no specific reporting. Therefore low tail odds each.

Upset check: No competing STRONG named announcement or scandal/flip elevating any other candidate. Sanity: GROAT follows Brady with a household-name headliner; Hart matches that profile and has explicit reporting linking him to the slot.

iterations.content:
All agents agree Kevin Hart is the answer. Evidence brief explicitly confirms Netflix announced Kevin Hart as next roast subject with Shane Gillis hosting. My search corroborates this. The disagreement is purely on MAGNITUDE — how high to push Kevin Hart (range 0.765-0.952).

Given direct, specific, named, confirmed announcement from Netflix with public confirmation from Hart himself, this should be very high. However, I'll temper slightly for resolution risk (could the roast be delayed/cancelled, or could another roast air first?). Mean of agents is ~0.86 for Kevin Hart.

args.reasoning: All 8 agents agree Kevin Hart is the answer, with disagreement only on magnitude (range 0.765-0.952, mean ~0.86). This is a MAGNITUDE disagreement, not factual. My search confirmed: Netflix officially announced "The Roast of Kevin Hart" as the next GROAT installment after Tom Brady, with Shane Gillis hosting, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at Kia Forum LA as part of Netflix Is a Joke Fest. Hart publicly confirmed on Instagram. Returning producers Jeff Ross and Casey Patterson are attached. This is a direct, named, confirmed announcement that resolves the question with high confidence. The only residual uncertainty is whether the roast actually airs as scheduled (cancellation/delay risk) or whether a different roast might air first — both low-probability scenarios given the public announcement and scheduled date. I'll place Kevin Hart at ~0.92, slightly above the agent mean but reflecting the strong confirmation. Distribute remaining ~8% across other named comedians/figures who could conceivably be alternates if plans changed, with floors at 0.02 minimum.
